context.title: SAP Acquires Reltio to Accelerate BDC Adoption via Cross-Enterprise MDM
context.core_question: Can SAP transform BDC from a struggling product into a credible enterprise AI data backbone by integrating Reltio's MDM capability across heterogeneous data estates?
context.current_state: BDC exists with Databricks partnership but suffers from 83% DSAG user unfamiliarity and painful mid-migration UX; Reltio acquisition adds AI entity resolution for cross-enterprise data harmonisation.
context.core_dilemma: Does acquiring Reltio accelerate BDC adoption by solving the external data fragmentation problem, or does it add integration complexity that deepens the migration crisis for existing SAP customers?
context.key_decision_point: Whether to position Reltio's MDM capability as a self-service data onboarding layer for BDC or as an internal harmonisation tool — each choice implies different go-to-market sequencing and customer risk.
target_outcomes: Grow BDC adoption beyond early adopters to the broader SAP customer base
Establish SAP as the enterprise AI data backbone for hybrid SAP/non-SAP estates
Reduce customer migration friction and eliminate mid-migration billing pain
hard_constraints: Multi-year ERP migration timelines for existing S/4HANA customers
DSAG trust deficit: 83% BDC unfamiliarity rate creates a significant awareness and credibility gap
Enterprise data gravity — customers cannot migrate all data to SAP systems
Competitive pressure from Salesforce Data Cloud and Microsoft Fabric for the unified data platform narrative
known_unknowns: Whether Reltio's integration will simplify or compound BDC migration complexity
Speed of competitive response from Salesforce and Microsoft
Whether Databricks alliance strengthens or weakens as SAP internalises more data stack capability

signals.name: AI Entity Resolution as Integration Moat

mechanism_conditions.activation_condition: Reltio integration is complete and entity resolution pipelines are exposed via BDC APIs
mechanism_conditions.expected_effect: Elevates BDC from a SAP-data-only layer to a cross-enterprise AI data backbone, expanding addressable use cases.

cascade_rules.trigger_condition: Reltio MDM layer reaches general availability on BDC within 12 months

signals.mechanism_note: Reltio's AI entity resolution produces a unified golden record from heterogeneous enterprise data. When embedded in BDC, this capability directly removes the primary technical barrier — data fragmentation — that has limited BDC adoption among enterprises with mixed SAP/non-SAP estates.

signals.name: BDC Adoption Gap — DSAG Trust Deficit

mechanism_conditions.binding_condition: 83% of DSAG members remain unfamiliar with BDC; customer trust anchors on existing ERP relationships
mechanism_conditions.release_condition: SAP demonstrates measurable migration simplification and cost reduction for at least 20% of BDC customers
mechanism_conditions.expected_effect: Constrains near-term BDC pipeline expansion until SAP closes the awareness and trust gap with its existing enterprise base.

cascade_rules.trigger_condition: DSAG awareness rate improves above 50% within 18 months post-acquisition

signals.mechanism_note: The DSAG research establishes a hard market signal: SAP's core customer base is not engaged with BDC. This constrains the pipeline available for Reltio-enabled upsell and forces SAP to invest in re-education and migration support before the Reltio premium can be realised.

signals.name: Mid-Migration Billing Friction — Churn Risk

mechanism_conditions.modulation_condition: Customers mid-migration are billed at full cloud rates for systems they cannot yet operationalise
mechanism_conditions.expected_effect: Dampens BDC expansion velocity by generating negative word-of-mouth and increasing churn risk among early BDC adopters.

signals.no_cascade_reason: direct local effect — billing friction is a steady-state constraint until SAP redesigns migration-phase pricing
signals.mechanism_note: Customers who are stuck mid-migration become involuntary proof-points against BDC adoption. The Binoy James quote ('that line is going to haunt SAP's sales deck') captures how this friction becomes a competitive liability that Reltio alone cannot fix.

signals.name: Databricks Alliance as Ecosystem Amplifier

mechanism_conditions.modulation_condition: Reltio MDM capability is exposed via Databricks integration points, extending BDC reach to data-first enterprises outside the core SAP customer base
mechanism_conditions.expected_effect: Amplifies the AI entity resolution driver by extending its reach to Databricks-anchored enterprise data ecosystems.

cascade_rules.trigger_condition: Databricks and Reltio integration roadmap is jointly announced at Sapphire 2026

signals.mechanism_note: The Databricks alliance pre-dates Reltio but is directly strengthened by it. If Reltio's entity resolution layer is surfaced through Databricks, SAP can approach data-first enterprises without requiring S/4HANA migration as a prerequisite — a significant strategic unlock.

high_leverage_unknowns: Whether Reltio's integration will simplify or compound BDC migration complexity
Speed of competitive response from Salesforce and Microsoft
Whether Databricks alliance strengthens or weakens as SAP internalises more data stack capability

assumptions_explicit.target_unknown: Whether Reltio's integration will simplify or compound BDC migration complexity
assumptions_explicit.assumption_text: Reltio's cloud-native architecture (Spanner/Google Cloud migration in 2024) is assumed to be compatible with BDC's integration layer without requiring a full data model rewrite.

assumptions_explicit.target_unknown: Speed of competitive response from Salesforce and Microsoft
assumptions_explicit.assumption_text: Salesforce Data Cloud and Microsoft Fabric will respond within 12–18 months with MDM-adjacent features, but SAP's ERP data moat preserves a 12-month window of differentiation.

assumptions_explicit.target_unknown: Whether Databricks alliance strengthens or weakens as SAP internalises more data stack capability
assumptions_explicit.assumption_text: Databricks is assumed to remain a committed distribution partner as long as BDC does not directly compete with Databricks' core lakehouse offering.
